perturbation.original_text: (d) Legally Required Disclosures . Nothing in this Agreement shall preclude Receiving Party from making any disclosure of Confidential Information that is required by applicable law or regulation or by a valid order of a court or other governmental body having jurisdiction, provided that Receiving Party uses best efforts to limit the scope of the required disclosure, provides notification to Disclosing Party of such requirement as soon as reasonably possible, and cooperates with Disclosing Party in seeking an appropriate protective order, confidential treatment, or similar remedy limiting the subsequent use and disclosure of any information required to be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: (j) Counterparts . This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts (including by facsimile or .pdf), each of which shall constitute an original and all of which, when taken together, shall constitute one agreement. Nothing in this Agreement shall preclude Receiving Party from making any disclosure of Confidential Information that is required by applicable law or regulation or by a valid order of a court or other governmental body having jurisdiction, provided that Receiving Party uses best efforts to limit the scope of the required disclosure, provides notification to Disclosing Party of such requirement as soon as reasonably possible, and cooperates with Disclosing Party in seeking an appropriate protective order, confidential treatment, or similar remedy limiting the subsequent use and disclosure of any information required to be disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Legally Required Disclosures' clause to the 'Counterparts' section makes it less visible and harder to find, potentially hindering a party's ability to invoke their right to disclose information under legal mandate, as the location is not intuitive for such a provision.
perturbation.law_explanation: 17 CFR § 229.601 specifies requirements for exhibits filed as part of securities regulations. This includes material contracts. While it doesn't explicitly mandate where disclosure exceptions must be placed, generally accepted legal practices and the SEC's emphasis on transparency suggest such vital clauses should be conspicuously placed within the main body or a clearly labeled section, not buried within boilerplate clauses like 'Counterparts'. This relocation reduces the clarity and accessibility required for compliance with securities laws.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 229.601 - (Item 601) Exhibits. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 229.601 - (Item 601) Exhibits. CFR Table of Popular Names § 229.601 (Item 601) Exhibits. Link to an amendment published at 89 FR 21914 , Mar. 28, 2024. This amendment was delayed indefinitely at 89 FR 25804 , Apr. 12, 2024. (a) Exhibits and index required. (1) Subject to Rule 411(c) ( § 230.411(c) of this chapter) under the Securities Act and Rule 12b-23(c) (§ 240.12b-23(c) of this chapter) under the Exchange Act regarding incorporation of exhibits by reference, the exhibits required in the exhibit table must be filed as indicated, as part of the registration statement or report. (2) Each registration statement or report shall contain an exhibit index, which must appear before the required signatures in the registration statement or report. For convenient reference, each exhibit shall be listed in the exhibit index according to the number assigned to it in the exhibit table. If an exhibit is incorporated by reference, this must be noted in the exhibit index. Each exhibit identified in the exhibit index (other than an exhibit filed in eXtensible Business Reporting Language or an exhibit that is filed with Form ABS-EE) must include an active link to an exhibit that is filed with the registration statement or report or, if the exhibit is incorporated by reference, an active hyperlink to the exhibit separately filed on EDGAR. If a registration statement or report is amended, each amendment must include hyperlinks to the exhibits required with the amendment. For a description of each of the exhibits included in the exhibit table, see paragraph (b) of this section. (3) This Item applies only to the forms specified in the exhibit table. With regard to forms not listed in that table, reference shall be made to the appropriate form for the specific exhibit filing requirements applicable thereto. (4) If
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the 'Legally Required Disclosures' clause to the 'Counterparts' section makes it less visible and harder to find, potentially hindering a party's ability to disclose information under legal mandate. The law explanation supports this by stating that generally accepted legal practices and the SEC's emphasis on transparency suggest such vital clauses should be conspicuously placed, not buried within boilerplate clauses. 17 CFR § 229.601 specifies requirements for exhibits filed as part of securities regulations, implying that material contracts should have clear exhibits and indexes. Although the law snippets do not explicitly state where the legally required disclosure must be located, the combination of the perturbation explanation, law explanation, and the law snippets suggests the relocation reduces clarity, which in turn weakens the legally required disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: (h) Both parties hereby acknowledges that in its review the other party’s Confidential Information it and its representatives will have access to material non-public information concerning this other party. Each Party acknowledges, that it and its representatives are aware, that the United States or other applicable securities laws prohibit any person, who has received from an issuer material non-public information relating to an issuer of securities, from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Use of Names; Publicity . Except as otherwise provided herein, nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as conferring any right on Receiving Party to use in any manner Disclosing Party’s name or any trade name or trademark. Receiving Party will make no public announcement or other public statement concerning the existence of this Agreement or the Parties’ respective performances hereunder without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, which may be withheld in Disclosing Party’s sole and absolute discretion, except as necessary to comply with applicable law or regulations. Both parties hereby acknowledges that in its review the other party’s Confidential Information it and its representatives will have access to material non-public information concerning this other party. Each Party acknowledges, that it and its representatives are aware, that the United States or other applicable securities laws prohibit any person, who has received from an issuer material non-public information relating to an issuer of securities, from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the acknowledgement regarding material non-public information to the 'Use of Names; Publicity' section buries a crucial warning about insider trading within a clause about publicity. This obfuscates the serious legal implications of misusing confidential information, potentially diminishing the enforceability of the insider trading prohibition.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 78j - Manipulative and deceptive devices
perturbation.law_explanation: 15 U.S. Code § 78j prohibits the use of manipulative and deceptive devices in connection with the purchase or sale of securities. This is the basis for insider trading laws. While the moved text acknowledges the prohibition of insider trading, placing it within a section about publicity reduces its prominence and clarity. A reasonable person may not readily associate the 'Use of Names; Publicity' section with the serious legal ramifications of insider trading, potentially weakening the enforceability of the prohibition and increasing the risk of non-compliance.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 78j - Manipulative and deceptive devices | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 78j - Manipulative and deceptive devices U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next It shall be unlawful for any person , directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce or of the mails, or of any facility of any national securities exchange— (a) (1) To effect a short sale , or to use or employ any stop-loss order in connection with the purchase or sale, of any security other than a government security, in contravention of such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate in the public interest or for the protection of investors. (2) Paragraph (1) of this subsection shall not apply to security futures products . (b) To use or employ, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security registered on a national securities exchange or any security not so registered, or any securities-based swap agreement [1] any manipulative or deceptive device or contrivance in contravention of such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate in the public interest or for the protection of investors. (c) (1) To effect, accept, or facilitate a transaction involving the loan or borrowing of securities in contravention of such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate in the public interest or for the protection of investors. (2) Nothing in paragraph (1) may be construed to limit the authority of the appropriate Federal banking agency (as defined in section 1813(q) of title 12 ), the National Credit Union Administration , or any other Federal department or agency having a responsibility under Federal law to prescribe rules or regulations restricting transactions involving the loan or borrowing of securities in or
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text contradicts the intention of securities laws, specifically 15 U.S. Code § 78j, by burying the acknowledgement of insider trading prohibitions within a section concerning publicity. As per the law explanation, this placement reduces the prominence and clarity of the insider trading warning. This contradicts the intent of the law, which seeks to prevent the use of manipulative and deceptive devices in connection with securities. The original text presented the acknowledgement directly, whereas the changed text obfuscates it, thereby weakening the enforceability of the prohibition. The scraped law snippets reinforce the illegality of using manipulative devices in securities trading.

perturbation.original_text: (f) Governing Law; Jurisdiction . This Agreement is made under and shall be construed according to the laws of the State of California without regard to any conflict of law principles that would provide for the application of the law of another jurisdiction. Any disputes under this Agreement may be brought in the state courts and the Federal courts located in the Northern District of California, and the parties hereby consent to the personal jurisdiction and exclusive venue of these courts.
perturbation.changed_text: (e) Notices . All notices or reports permitted or required under this Agreement will be in writing and will be sent by personal delivery or reputable expedited delivery service with signature required. All such notices or reports will be deemed given upon receipt. Notices will be sent to the addresses set forth at the beginning of this Agreement, in this section, or such other addresses as either Party may specify in writing. Governing Law; Jurisdiction . This Agreement is made under and shall be construed according to the laws of the State of California without regard to any conflict of law principles that would provide for the application of the law of another jurisdiction. Any disputes under this Agreement may be brought in the state courts and the Federal courts located in the Northern District of California, and the parties hereby consent to the personal jurisdiction and exclusive venue of these courts.
perturbation.explanation: By inserting the 'Governing Law; Jurisdiction' clause into the 'Notices' section, the governing law and jurisdiction provisions are obfuscated. This could lead to disputes over which state's laws apply and where legal proceedings should occur, as this vital provision is not prominently displayed. This ambiguity can hinder enforcement.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Code of Civil Procedure § 410.42 deals with choice of forum clauses. While not explicitly requiring its placement, the standard legal interpretation calls for it to be conspicuously presented. Embedding the jurisdiction clause within the notices section might make it less obvious, potentially rendering it harder to enforce, as its presence might be overlooked. This obscurity challenges the intention of ensuring clarity and mutual understanding regarding jurisdictional matters.

(a) The following provisions of a contract between the contractor and a subcontractor with principal offices in this state, for the construction of a public or private work of improvement in this state, shall be void and unenforceable: (1) A provision which purports to require any dispute between the parties to be litigated, arbitrated, or otherwise determined outside this state. (2) A provision which purports to preclude a party from commencing such a proceeding or obtaining a judgment or other resolution in this state or the courts of this state. (b) For purposes of this section, “construction” means any work or services performed on, or materials provided for, a work of improvement, as defined in Section 8050 of the Civil Code, and for which a lien may be claimed pursuant to Section 8400 of the Civil Code (whether or not a lien is in fact cl
perturbation.justification: The original contract had a clearly defined section for 'Governing Law; Jurisdiction'. By inserting this clause within the 'Notices' section in the changed text, it obfuscates its importance and could lead to disputes over which state's laws apply. This aligns with the perturbation definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction', where a required legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section, making it difficult to locate or interpret. The Law Explanation states that embedding the jurisdiction clause within the notices section might make it less obvious, potentially rendering it harder to enforce. CCP 410.42 does not explicitly mandate a specific placement, the law explanation indicates that the standard legal interpretation calls for it to be conspicuously presented. The modified text creates a structural flaw that weakens the enforceability of the governing law and jurisdiction clause because it is not prominently displayed.
